mechanism_narrative: Increases in the federal funds rate directly raise the discount rate for equity valuations, compressing price-to-earnings multiples and generating negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns show positive mean returns when FEDFUNDS increases, or Rank IC remains below 0.02 over 3-year rolling windows.

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate exceeds 6.0%, labor market slack triggers a recessionary pricing regime that systematically reduces corporate earnings expectations, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Forward returns remain positive in >55% of periods where UNRATE exceeds 6.0%, or the signal loses statistical significance (p > 0.10) in out-of-sample testing.

mechanism_narrative: The joint occurrence of rising retail sales and falling federal funds rates creates a liquidity-fueled demand expansion that drives positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: The interaction term between RSAFS and FEDFUNDS yields a negative or insignificant coefficient in multivariate regression, or forward returns are negative in >60% of triggered instances.

mechanism_narrative: Accelerating industrial production signals strengthening supply-side capacity utilization that supports near-term corporate revenue growth, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: INDPRO acceleration fails to predict positive 5-day returns during early-cycle expansions, or the signal predictive power vanishes when GDP growth exceeds 3.0% annually.

mechanism_narrative: Sustained growth in total nonfarm payrolls reflects expanding economic activity and consumer spending capacity, which directly translates to positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: PAYEMS growth shows no statistically significant correlation with 20-day forward returns, or Rank IC falls below 0.01 across all rolling 5-year windows.

mechanism_narrative: When the consumer price index exceeds 230.0, persistent inflation forces central banks to maintain restrictive policy stances, increasing equity risk premiums and producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Forward returns are positive in >50% of periods where CPIAUCSL exceeds 230.0, or the threshold effect disappears when controlling for Fed funds rate changes.

mechanism_narrative: The combination of rising housing starts and declining unemployment indicates synchronized strength in consumer durables demand and labor market tightness, driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: The joint signal fails to outperform baseline returns, or the interaction term loses predictive significance when housing inventory levels exceed historical averages.

mechanism_narrative: Quarterly GDP growth exceeding 4.0% signals an overheating economy that triggers mean-reversion dynamics and profit-taking, resulting in negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: GDPC1 growth above 4.0% consistently predicts positive forward returns, or the negative relationship reverses during periods of high fiscal stimulus.
